revision_notes: AutoEval Execution Summary: AutoEval execution failed. Build status: FAILED. Build ID: CodeExecutionEnvironment:b8b8d385-edf2-48dc-9398-5bf767b6f5e5.
difficulty_check_result: TRIVIAL — requires at least MEDIUM (collapsed from HARD in revision_1)
agent_review: WARNING — pytest still installed in Dockerfile (via uv venv) not test.sh; test.sh not self-contained; plus a suggestion to add an orientation sentence to instruction.md

evaluations.note: AutoEval run against the REVISED zip; produced the TRIVIAL difficulty verdict captured here

image_builds_evidence: oracle 3/3 and all 14 agent trials executed with every test 10/10 — agents cannot run unless the image built
build_failed_string_interpretation: Per standing memo 'AutoEval Build FAILED usually means UNSOLVABLE/failed eval gate, not a broken image': the 'Build status: FAILED' note is the generic wrapper for a failed AutoEval gate. This round the gate fails because difficulty=TRIVIAL (below the MEDIUM floor), NOT because of a Docker compile/layer error.
note: ONE real blocker this round, and it is a regression: difficulty collapsed from HARD (revision_1) to TRIVIAL. The submitter revised the zip to clear every revision_1 instruction-sufficiency gap (bare case names, per-column [2,3,5]/[1,-1,2] constants in instruction.md AND operations.md, cargo-on-PATH note, uv-based pytest install) — but over-disclosing the packed-path constants removed the difficulty cliff, so both models now solve it 100%. There is NO separate Docker build to fix; the fix is to walk back the constant disclosure to restore >= MEDIUM difficulty.
tmp_bundle_path_note: stb 2.2.2 writes the bundle under /tmp/claude-1000/feedback_<id>_<ISO8601>Z, NOT /tmp/feedback_<id>_* as personal_docs/feedback.md documents.
